title: Saganaki
subtitle: Pan-fried Greek cheese with lemon
excerpt: Saganaki is a Greek dish of thick cheese slices quickly pan-fried until a golden crust forms on the outside while the inside softens. It takes less than five minutes. Halloumi is the most practical choice in the UK; a squeeze of lemon on the hot cheese is the only garnish it needs.
sourceNotes: Saganaki takes its name from the small two-handled frying pan (saganaki) in which it is traditionally cooked. The dish appears across Greece and Cyprus using a range of firm, high-melting cheeses including graviera, kefalograviera, and halloumi. Halloumi is the easiest to find outside Greece.

content.text: Saganaki is a thick slice of firm, high-melting cheese fried in a very hot pan until deeply golden on both sides and soft in the centre. It is one of the quickest things in Greek cooking and one of the most satisfying. The cheese needs to be at room temperature before frying so the outside colours before the inside melts and runs out.
Pat the halloumi slices dry with kitchen paper. Dust lightly with plain flour on both sides, shaking off any excess.

Heat {{olive-oil}} in a small frying pan over high heat until shimmering. Lay the halloumi slices in the pan in a single layer. Fry for 2 minutes until a deep golden crust forms, then flip and fry for 2 minutes more. The cheese should be golden and slightly softened but still holding its shape.
Transfer immediately to a plate. Squeeze lemon juice over the hot cheese, grind over some black pepper, and serve at once. A drizzle of honey works well if you have it.

items.symptom: The cheese sticks to the pan
items.cause: Pan not hot enough, or cheese not floured
items.fix: The pan must be very hot before the cheese goes in. Dust lightly with flour, which helps form the crust quickly and prevents sticking.

items.symptom: The cheese melts and pools in the pan
items.cause: Cheese was too warm, or sliced too thin
items.fix: Chill the cheese first for 15 minutes. Slice at least 1 cm thick. High-melting cheese like halloumi is more forgiving than softer varieties.

items.symptom: The crust is pale rather than golden
items.fix: Saganaki needs a hot pan. If the cheese is not colouring within 90 seconds, increase the heat. Work quickly.

content.text: Saganaki is a mezze dish and a taverna staple. It arrives on the table as soon as you sit down in many Greek restaurants. In some Greek-American restaurants it is flambéed at the table with brandy; the flaming is theatrical, not traditional. At home, just the hot pan, the golden crust, and the lemon is enough.
